modified_files.change: Add SizeBytes uint64 to SplitCommitEntry; extend Encode/Decode for the new field.
modified_files.reason: bugfix: SplitCommitEntry had no way to carry new-file size for catalog-record reconstruction

modified_files.change: Extend SplitCommit round-trip subtest to cover SizeBytes.

ExecuteSplitAtomic: compute per-new-path content length and cat.Put a fresh CatalogRecord (Status=StatusActive) for every new fileID inside the same WAL-covered apply closure. RecoverSplitCommits: mirror the same cat.Put loop from payload.Entries on replay.
modified_files.reason: bugfix: new fileIDs produced by a split had no catalog.CatalogRecord at all, making them permanently unreadable/unappendable via ContentStore.Read/Append (both require cat.Get to succeed first)

modified_files.change: assertFullSplitApplied: add assertion that cat.Get(newFileID) succeeds with Status=StatusActive and expected SizeBytes.
modified_files.reason: regression coverage for the bugfix, exercised by both TestSplitAtomicCommit's live-path and its 4 crash-injection/recovery subtests

backward_compatibility: SplitCommitEntry field addition is append-only in the wire format (new 8 bytes appended per entry after existing FileID field); no existing on-disk WAL records from prior runs need to be read back in this dev-only pre-release repo (no persisted state carried across commits in test/CI). No public API signatures change (ExecuteSplitAtomic, RecoverSplitCommits keep identical signatures).
risk: Fix touches the already-verified issue #12 capstone (2b.3.6) and issue #13 (2b.4.1) commit paths; must re-run full split/wal/catalog test suites, not just the new race test, to confirm zero regression.
out_of_scope_explicitly_not_done: Wiring engine/split.Trigger into catalog.ContentStore.Append (pre-existing, disclosed, deliberately deferred gap; not required by issue #14's acceptance criteria, which the test harness works around per architecture-discovery.md)
PathHash population for new catalog records (left 0, matching this repo's existing convention of not actively computing PathHash outside of explicit test fixtures)
